steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The passage marks two events with explicit years: 2014 (Gomez won a competition) and 2017 (Gomez created a show). The three-year gap between the events is the key signal — the blank must introduce the second sentence in a way that expresses that the second event came after the first.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Instead,': Signals contrast or replacement — creating a show is not presented as an alternative to winning a competition, so this transition misrepresents the relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — 'For example,': Signals that the second sentence illustrates the first — but the 2017 show is a new, distinct event, not an example of winning a competition. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Later,': Directly signals chronological progression, fitting the 2014-to-2017 time gap and expressing that the show came after the competition win. ✓
  • Choice D — 'In other words,': Signals restatement of the same idea — but the two sentences describe distinct events, not the same event rephrased. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
